replies.author: @SammaclauseGamgee
replies.text:
​@cliffordterrell5524 Superman has fights where innocent people could be hurt all the time; that's one of the best ways to challenge a hero who is almost completely indestructible. The issue was how bleak it was, imo. I really wish Henry Cavill got to be Superman in an actual Superman movie.

My biggest problem with Gunn's movie was that Superman was never all that challenged, and the moral crisis he faced was such an obvious no brainer that he didn't even really have a character arc. The one time he was challenged was fighting Ultraman and he didn't even try to help him, which I think Superman would do, even if he couldn't save him in the end; Ultraman was made by Lex, mentally regarded, and being controlled by him. Gunn even used him for a joke during their last fight that made him look sympathetic (one his mask was off, the movie made him look like a very unfortunate simpleton who was just being led in the wrong direction), but Superman didn't even try. I think that wouldve been the perfect way to show what Superman is: an optimist who /wants/ to save everyone, but can't always do it for reasons beyond his control. Instead, they gave that to the Kaiju....but it's a Kaiju. And he didn't try very hard with that, either.

replies.text:
The Facts as of 2026
To give you the update that video missed:

The Iron Eyes Case: Chase Iron Eyes (the father) officially retracted his petition in August 2024, stating he acted on a witness's "bad information" and that there was no evidence of grooming [5.2, 5.4].

The Massachusetts Order: The harassment order involving a 12-year-old was dissolved in June 2023. Miller’s lawyers stated the order was "used as a weapon" by someone seeking fame [3.1, 3.6].

The "Victim" Testimonials: Several people who were supposedly "kidnapped" by Miller have since come forward to say Miller actually saved them from domestic abuse situations, which is exactly what the article you shared highlights.
